content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Kristen E. McMahon 
S & FA 
August 19, 1988 
1101 King street, suite 600 
Alexandria, VA 
22314 
Re: 
88-319 
Dear Ms. McMahon: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on August 15, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to the information needed. 
If your request is 
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we 
can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. 
Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
'\ 
>t~-",-/ YVI' .A 
,t-j. 
// / 
~~eanne Pritchard 
Chief 
Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


State and Federal Associates, Inc. 
Kevin S. Braaten-Moen 
Political Reform Consultant 
1101 King Street, Suite 600, Alexandria, Virginia 22314 
(703) 739-0200 
August 10, 1988 
FPPC Technical Assistance/Analysis Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, California 95814 
Dear Mr. Braaten-Moen, 
We 
are in the process of compiling information for our 1988 edition 
of 
S&FA's 
Executive's 
Handbook 
on 
Political 
Contributions. 
This 
publication--updated three times a year--outlines the major provisions of 
each 
state's 
campaign 
contributions 
law, 
with 
an 
emphasis 
on 
contributions 
limitations/prohibitions 
and disclosure 
requirements 
for 
corporations, corporate political action committees, individuals, and labor 
unions. 
I 
have enclosed a copy of the California entry. Please review the 
summary 
and 
check lists for accuracy and make any changes reflecting your 
current 
political contributions requirements. We would also appreciate 
receiving a 
copy of your current campaign finance law. 
Mr. Braaten-Moen, please feel free to call us if you have a question or 
comment 
about 
the 
state entry. I have 
enclosed 
an 
addressed 
stamped 
envelope for your convenience in returning the updated materials. 
Thank you for your efforts in assisting us with the 1988 edition of this 
pUblication. 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Kristen E. McMahon 
Publications Assistant

embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission Kristen E. McMahon S & FA August 19, 1988 1101 King street, suite 600 Alexandria, VA 22314 Re: 88-319 Dear Ms. McMahon: Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act was received on August 15, 1988 by the Fair Political Practices commission. If you have any questions about your advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the information needed. If your request is for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) You also should be aware that your letter and our response are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. JP:plh Very truly yours, '\ >t~-",-/ YVI' .A ,t-j. // / ~~eanne Pritchard Chief Technical Assistance and Analysis Division 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 State and Federal Associates, Inc. Kevin S. Braaten-Moen Political Reform Consultant 1101 King Street, Suite 600, Alexandria, Virginia 22314 (703) 739-0200 August 10, 1988 FPPC Technical Assistance/Analysis Division 428 J Street, Suite 800 Sacramento, California 95814 Dear Mr. Braaten-Moen, We are in the process of compiling information for our 1988 edition of S&FA's Executive's Handbook on Political Contributions. This publication--updated three times a year--outlines the major provisions of each state's campaign contributions law, with an emphasis on contributions limitations/prohibitions and disclosure requirements for corporations, corporate political action committees, individuals, and labor unions. I have enclosed a copy of the California entry. Please review the summary and check lists for accuracy and make any changes reflecting your current political contributions requirements. We would also appreciate receiving a copy of your current campaign finance law. Mr. Braaten-Moen, please feel free to call us if you have a question or comment about the state entry. I have enclosed an addressed stamped envelope for your convenience in returning the updated materials. Thank you for your efforts in assisting us with the 1988 edition of this pUblication. Enclosure Sincerely, Kristen E. McMahon Publications Assistant
